channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Geopolitical news headlines curated from Tier 1 sources

item.title: ⛽ US Gasoline Prices Near Four-Year High as Iran War Disrupts Supply
item.description: Average US pump prices have reached their highest level in nearly four years following the US-Israel attack on Iran, reflecting the ongoing energy crisis. The American Automobile Association data shows sustained pressure on fuel costs.

item.title: 🛢️ BP Profit Doubles to $3.2 Billion on Iran War Oil Trading
item.description: British oil major BP reported first-quarter profit of $3.2 billion, beating expectations by 20% and marking its highest profit since 2023. The Iran war boosted oil trading results significantly.

item.title: 🏭 European Aluminium Premium Doubles as Iran War Disrupts Supply
item.description: The premium for aluminium billet in Europe has doubled since the Iran war began, with two months of disrupted Middle East supply squeezing construction and transport industries.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Over 1,100 Ukrainians Accused of Betrayal, One in Five Are Minors
item.description: A Reuters investigation reveals that since Russia's full-scale invasion, more than 1,100 Ukrainians have been accused of betrayal through arson, terrorism or sabotage, with 20% being minors. The case of one teenager illustrates how children can be both perpetrator and victim.

item.title: 🇨🇳 Groups Petition US Trade Agency to Combat Forced Labor Imports
item.description: Pro-tariff and human rights groups asked the Trump administration to impose new import bans, duties and quotas targeting forced labor practices in China and other countries, pressing trading partners to adopt US-style enforcement.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukraine reveals details of Russia's new S-71K cruise missile
item.description: Ukrainian military intelligence disclosed specifications of Russia's new air-launched S-71K "Kovyor" cruise missile, including its design and foreign components. The revelation comes as Kyiv continues to expose Moscow's weapons supply chains.

item.title: 🇦🇪🛢️ UAE plans to produce 5 million barrels per day after OPEC exit
item.description: UAE Energy Minister Suhail Al Mazrouei announced plans to produce 5 million barrels of oil daily after leaving OPEC and OPEC+, marking a dramatic shift in global energy dynamics as the Iran war disrupts Gulf oil markets.

item.title: 🇩🇪🇺🇦 German defense minister says Ukraine partnership benefits Germany
item.description: German Defense Minister Boris Pistorius highlighted how Germany benefits from defense cooperation with Ukraine, noting Kyiv's rapid development of battlefield-tested technologies that enhance German military capabilities.

item.title: 🇬🇧💷 UK borrowing costs hit highest level since 2008
item.description: UK government borrowing costs closed at their highest level since 2008, driven by economic uncertainty and fiscal concerns under Prime Minister Keir Starmer's government amid global market volatility from the Iran conflict.

item.title: 🇦🇲🇦🇿 Armenia and Azerbaijan maintain high military spending despite peace deal
item.description: Despite agreeing to a provisional peace agreement last August, both Armenia and Azerbaijan continue to maintain some of the world's highest military spending as a percentage of GDP, reflecting an ongoing "peace-through-strength" approach.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran's Revolutionary Guards Seize Wartime Power, Blunting Supreme Leader's Role
item.description: Two months into the war with the US and Israel, Iran no longer has a single undisputed clerical arbiter at the pinnacle of power, a break with the past that may be hardening Tehran's stance in negotiations with Washington.

item.title: 🇮🇷 US Spy Agencies Examine How Iran Would React if Trump Declares Victory
item.description: US intelligence agencies are analyzing scenarios for how Iran might respond if President Donald Trump declares victory and seeks to end the conflict, exploring potential Iranian reactions to various US policy approaches.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Trump Says Iran Wants US to Open Strait of Hormuz 'As Soon As Possible'
item.description: President Donald Trump stated that Iran has expressed its desire for the United States to open the Strait of Hormuz as soon as possible, as the vital shipping channel remains blocked amid ongoing hostilities.

item.title: 🚢 VLCC Tanker Attempts to Cross Strait of Hormuz
item.description: A VLCC tanker named Idemitsu Maru attempted to cross the Strait of Hormuz, according to shipping data, in one of the first major vessel movements through the blocked waterway since the conflict began.

item.title: 🏛️ Purdue Pharma Executives Sentenced in OxyContin Criminal Case
item.description: Executives of Purdue Pharma, the maker of OxyContin, received criminal sentences as part of a settlement over the opioid crisis that devastated communities across the United States.

item.title: 🏛️ Former FBI Director Comey Indicted for Second Time
item.description: Former FBI Director James Comey has been indicted for a second time, CNN reported, marking another legal development involving the former Trump-era FBI chief.

item.title: 🛢️ UAE to Quit OPEC in Blow to World's Leading Oil Exporters
item.description: The United Arab Emirates will withdraw from OPEC effective May 1, dealing a significant blow to the oil cartel as the Iran war creates unprecedented energy market disruption and exposes divisions among Gulf producers.

item.title: 🤖 Google Signs Classified AI Deal With Pentagon for Military Applications
item.description: Alphabet's Google joined a growing list of technology firms signing deals with the U.S. Department of Defense to use AI models for classified work. The move marks a significant expansion of tech-military collaboration amid the Iran conflict.

item.title: 🇸🇦🤝 Gulf Leaders Hold Emergency Meeting in Saudi Arabia on Iran War Crisis
item.description: Saudi Arabia hosted an emergency consultative meeting with other Persian Gulf leaders in Jeddah to discuss the ongoing Iran conflict and its regional implications. The summit reflects growing coordination among Gulf states amid the Hormuz standoff.

item.title: 🇪🇺💼 EU Trade Chief Resigns After Clashing Over Hasty Trump Deal
item.description: Sabine Weyand, who led the European Commission's trade department for seven years, is leaving after clashing with superiors over a hastily agreed trade deal with U.S. President Donald Trump. The departure highlights tensions in EU-US trade relations.

item.title: ⚔️🚁 US Military Expands Secret Boat Strike Operations with More Aircraft
item.description: The U.S. military has quietly increased the number of secret fixed-wing attack aircraft and armed MQ-9 Reaper drones for boat strike missions in the Iran conflict, according to officials. The expansion reflects the ongoing maritime dimension of the Hormuz standoff.

item.title: 🇺🇦🇷 Israel Says No Proof Russian Grain Shipment Was Stolen as Ukraine Threatens Sanctions
item.description: Israel stated it found no evidence that a Russian grain shipment intercepted by Ukraine was stolen, contradicting Kyiv's claims and highlighting tensions between the allies over sanctions enforcement.

item.title: 🇮🇩 Indonesians Question Lebanon Peacekeeping Mission as Casualties Grow
item.description: Indonesian public support for the UN peacekeeping mission in Lebanon is wavering as casualties mount, with families and lawmakers questioning the value of continued participation in the dangerous operation.

item.title: 🇫🇷 France Urges Israel to Lift Gaza Aid Restrictions Amid Dire Conditions
item.description: France called on Israel to lift restrictions on humanitarian aid to Gaza, warning of increasingly dire conditions for civilians in the Palestinian territory after months of blockade.

item.title: 🇻🇪 Venezuela Accuses Polymarket of Insider Trading in Maduro Raid Betting
item.description: Venezuelan officials accused prediction market platform Polymarket of enabling insider trading after a soldier reportedly bet $400,000 on the outcome of a raid to capture President Nicolas Maduro.
